goal: Validate whether the microstructure feature family (primarily cf_micro_imbalance_1h) provides a genuine overlay alpha over the immutable base through a disciplined veto-first, lockbox-verified pipeline.
global_constraints: Immutable base active-signal-v1@1 must never be modified or re-tuned
Only microstructure_proxies family is active; all other feature families are locked

Do not reopen closed directions: leadership/breadth as directional edge, path-asymmetry, regime-disagreement, event-only overlays, trade-quality overlays without new information layer, derivatives positioning without real OI/liquidation/basis data
Lockbox overlay comparison (base vs base+overlay on same window) is mandatory before any strong conclusion
Simpler methods must be tested before complex models (bucket rule → threshold → logistic → CatBoost)
Short side is tested before long side at every stage
Veto overlay must be proven before early-cut or directional scout routes are unlocked
net_pnl > 0 must not be used as veto label — too noisy
Feature set limited to micro-core + minimal context support; no re-injection of old large feature packs
No feature/label/threshold selection on lockbox window; lockbox is truth-only

stages.title: Closure-audit v9 and status correction
stages.objective: Formally close v9 with honest status, open v10 project, register immutable baseline reference, and set up research map axes and open-items tracker.
actions: Open new research_project for v10
Record v9 achievements and unproven claims in research_memory with corrected status 'signal discovered, route unproven'
Lock immutable reference: snapshot_id=active-signal-v1, version=1, symbol=BTCUSDT, anchor=1h, execution=5m
Initialize research_map axes: route, side, feature_group, label_family, model_stage, final_decision
Create open-items register covering all v10 work items
success_criteria: Clean v10 project exists with corrected v9 status
Research map axes initialized
Open-items register created with all tracked items

stages.title: Materialization and dataset hardening for micro-core
stages.objective: Eliminate technical debt around 7 microstructure features by verifying stable materialization, correct 5m→1h alignment, absence of future leakage, and locking one working dataset version for v10.
actions: Verify all 7 microstructure features are published and stably materialized
Check full coverage, warmup behavior, null-rates, 5m→1h alignment, and absence of future leakage
Freeze one working dataset version for v10
If materialization is unstable, fix pipeline before expanding family
success_criteria: All 7 features pass: published, materialized, warmup_ok, null_rate_ok, alignment_ok, leakage_ok
Single dataset version locked for v10

stages.title: Augmented trade atlas with micro-core
stages.objective: Enrich base trade atlas with microstructure features at entry time, build analytical cuts (long/short, winners/losers, stop-loss clusters, worst-decile, weak-window, time-limit), and identify 3–5 strongest empirical patterns.
actions: Enrich trade atlas with micro-features at entry time and optionally first 1h after entry (causal only)
Build cuts: long vs short, winners vs losers, stop-loss cluster, worst-decile, weak-window, time-limit trades
Check monotonicity for cf_micro_imbalance_1h, side asymmetry, and whether signal is stronger on short veto
Save 3–5 strongest empirical patterns
success_criteria: Trade atlas enriched with micro-core features
Key cuts analyzed with documented monotonicity and asymmetry findings
3–5 strongest empirical patterns recorded

stages.title: Freeze improved veto labels
stages.objective: Define and freeze six meaningful veto label variants across three families (pnl, fragile_mae, composite) with balanced/conservative modes, locking definitions before any training begins.
actions: Define bad_trade_pnl_balanced and bad_trade_pnl_conservative labels
Define fragile_trade_mae_balanced and fragile_trade_mae_conservative labels
Define bad_trade_composite_balanced and bad_trade_composite_conservative labels
For each label document: what counts as bad trade, ambiguous cases, material harm threshold
Lock all label definitions — prohibit changes after training starts
success_criteria: Six label variants frozen with documented definitions
No net_pnl>0 used as veto label
Label lock enforced going forward

stages.title: Simple baselines before CatBoost
stages.objective: Test whether a complex model layer is even needed by comparing four increasingly sophisticated baselines (bucket veto, threshold veto, logistic, small CatBoost) on selection OOS using micro-core features.
actions: Baseline A: single-feature bucket veto — remove trades from worst micro-imbalance bucket
Baseline B: single-feature threshold veto — remove trades crossing fixed threshold on cf_micro_imbalance_1h
Baseline C: logistic regression on 3–5 micro-core features
Baseline D: small CatBoost on micro-core with limited depth, no wide HPO
Compare each baseline on selection delta PnL, delta PF, delta DD, removed trades, false veto rate
success_criteria: All four baselines evaluated on selection OOS
Clear ranking of baseline methods documented
Decision recorded on whether CatBoost complexity is justified

stages.title: Short-first veto models
stages.objective: Train short-side veto models on frozen labels using micro-core and minimal context, selecting the best short route by selection economics rather than AUC alone.
actions: Train short veto models on labels: short-pnl, short-fragile, short-composite
Use only micro-core features plus minimal context support
Avoid wide HPO — keep model simple
Select best short route by selection economic effect (delta PnL, PF, DD) not just AUC
success_criteria: Short veto models trained across three label families
Best short route identified by selection economics
Model promoted to lockbox candidate or documented why not

stages.title: Long veto models
stages.objective: Train long-side veto models on frozen labels and compare against short-side results; do not promote long route if it remains noticeably weaker without economic compensation.
actions: Train long veto models on labels: long-pnl, long-fragile, long-composite
Compare long models against short-side results
Do not promote long route further if noticeably weaker than short without economic compensation
success_criteria: Long veto models trained across three label families
Long vs short comparison documented
Decision recorded on whether long route advances to lockbox

stages.title: Lockbox overlay test against immutable base
stages.objective: Run the decisive lockbox comparison: immutable base vs base+overlay for each surviving veto model, measuring delta PnL, PF, DD, removed-trade quality, weak-window repair, and ownership.
actions: Freeze thresholds on selection OOS for best surviving models
Build snapshot/overlay workflow
Run: immutable base, base+short veto, base+long veto, base+combined veto (if both sides alive)
Compute: delta PnL, delta PF, delta DD, removed trades, PnL of removed trades, share of removed winners, weak-window repair, ownership
success_criteria: Lockbox comparison completed for all surviving overlay configurations
Delta metrics computed for PnL, PF, DD, removed trades, winner share, weak-window repair, ownership
Clear lockbox pass/fail determination for each overlay

stages.title: Gate for early-cut and directional scout
stages.objective: Evaluate whether lockbox results justify unlocking at most one optional route (early-cut or directional scout) based on strict criteria; keep both locked if evidence is insufficient.
actions: Evaluate early-cut gate: veto route alive AND losers recognized by early micro-behavior
Evaluate directional scout gate: monotonicity confirmed, veto overlay at least watchlist-quality, feature shows entry-timing potential
Open at most one of the two routes
Record unlock decision with justification
success_criteria: Gate evaluation completed for both routes
At most one route opened with documented justification
Gate decision recorded in research_map
stages.gate_hint: Neither route may open if lockbox overlay did not reach at least watchlist quality

stages.title: Optional route execution
stages.objective: Execute only the route that genuinely earned opening — either early-cut (post-entry micro-features + minimal model) or directional scout (simple rule first, then small model) — and test against base on lockbox.
actions: If early_cut opened: build post-entry micro-features, train minimal model, test against base on lockbox
If directional_scout opened: test simple microstructure direction rule first, then small model, compare standalone and strict additive
If neither opened: skip this stage entirely
success_criteria: Opened route executed with selection and lockbox quality checks
Delta vs base assessed for meaningfulness
Final decision recorded for the route
stages.gate_hint: Only executes if stage_9 opened at least one route; skipped entirely if both routes remain locked

stages.title: Final v10 verdict
stages.objective: Deliver an honest terminal verdict on the microstructure family: PROMOTE if working overlay proven, WATCHLIST if partial result, REJECT if lockbox failed — with clear next steps.
actions: Aggregate all stage results into final assessment
Classify microstructure family outcome: Success (working overlay), Partial (watchlist-quality only), or Failed (lockbox rejected)
Record final route classification and next step in research_memory and research_map
Close v10 open-items register
success_criteria: Final route classification assigned (PROMOTE / WATCHLIST / REJECT)
Clear next step defined and documented

warnings: Plan is entirely in Russian; all objectives translated to English for semantic draft
Stages 9 (gate) and 10 (optional route) are gated branches that may produce no further work
v9 status was 'PROMISING SIGNAL DISCOVERED' not 'SUCCESS' — this plan inherits unproven microstructure signal
